title: cmate-orchestrate UAT report v1
description: Report printed to stdout and written to uat-report.json by the cmate-orchestrate UAT runner (scripts/uat.mjs). Closed schema: an unknown field is a contract violation, not something a reader may ignore. It records one UAT phase over the verification-passed issues of an already-delivered plan: either a read-only acceptance assessment (write_uat) or a bounded fix loop (fix_uat) that, when UAT fails, creates fix worktrees, dispatches a fix, re-verifies and re-merges, and repeats up to a fixed attempt cap. Reaching the cap with issues still failing is reported as blocked — never rounded up to success. Each attempt is appended to the history; a prior attempt is never overwritten.

phase.description: The single phase this invocation enabled. write_uat runs the acceptance assessment once and writes the report (read-only). fix_uat runs the bounded fix loop (mutating when approved). Exactly one is ever enabled.

status.description: success: UAT passed for every eligible issue (possibly after a fix). partial: UAT failed and the run stopped without exhausting the attempt cap (a preview, or a fix that could not be attempted). blocked: the attempt cap was reached with issues still failing UAT — an honest stop, not a failure. failure: nothing could be attempted (preflight failed, bad invocation).

stop_reason.description: Why the phase stopped. completed: every eligible issue passed UAT (possibly after a fix), or there was nothing eligible to do. max_attempts_reached: the fix loop hit its cap with failures remaining (status blocked). The rest name the first blocking condition; a failure is never reported as completed.

approved.description: Whether --approve was given. When false the fix loop is a no-mutation preview: no fix worktree is created, no fix worker is dispatched, and nothing is re-merged.

mutated.description: Whether at least one mutating command (git worktree add, commandmate send, git merge) was actually invoked. Always false when approved is false, and always false for write_uat.

out_dir.description: Directory the UAT artifacts were written to. Null when nothing was written.

max_attempts.description: The fixed upper bound on fix attempts. The loop never exceeds it; reaching it with failures remaining is blocked, not success.

attempts_used.description: How many fix attempts were actually performed. Never greater than max_attempts. Always 0 for write_uat and for a preview.

eligible_issues.description: Issues drawn from the dispatch report whose worker completed AND whose verification passed. Only these are ever subjected to UAT or repaired: an unverified issue is never fixed or re-merged.

preflight.description: Read-only preflight probes run before any mutation (commandmate availability, repo access, base resolvable). A blocking failure yields status failure and nothing is attempted.

attempts.description: The append-only attempt history. Index 0 is the initial assessment; each later entry is one fix attempt followed by a re-assessment. A prior attempt is never overwritten. Every entry records the UAT outcome that opened it, the fix worktrees created, the fixes dispatched, and the re-merge.

unresolved_issues.description: Issues still failing UAT when the run stopped. Non-empty exactly when status is blocked (cap reached) or partial (a preview or a fix that could not be attempted).

next_actions.description: What to do next and who owns it. Always present; empty only on an unqualified success.

git_sha_nullable.description: A resolved commit SHA, or null before one was resolved. A symbolic ref alone is never enough to record a base.

worktree_path.description: Repository-relative, or a single leading '../' for the sibling-worktree convention. No absolute path, no drive letter, no backslash, no control character. The dispatch path-escape guard additionally forbids any inner '..'; a value that fails it is never recorded here.

report_schema_version.description: Version of the UAT report the acceptance run produced. Null when UAT did not run (a preview). An unknown version is treated as a fail, never a pass.

outcome.description: Measured, never rounded. not_run only in a preview.

scenarios.description: Redacted names of the acceptance scenarios that were checked. Never a raw terminal or a secret.

worktree_result.description: One fix worktree, in the shape of the cmate-worktree-setup worktree-result (#1448): the branch is created from a re-confirmed base SHA, an existing worktree is never implicitly overwritten, and no absolute path is recorded.

reused.description: Always false here: a fix worktree is created fresh per attempt. created and reused are never both true.

worker_state.description: completed is worker completion only; the re-verification below is a separate gate.

outcome.description: merged: every fix branch re-merged cleanly. conflict: a re-merge hit a conflict and the loop stopped. skipped: nothing re-merged because no fix re-verified. not_attempted: a preview, or the phase stopped before re-merge.

kind.description: assess: this attempt only ran UAT (index 0, a preview, or the terminal assessment that reached the cap). fix: this attempt ran UAT and then performed a fix.

fix_performed.description: Whether this attempt created worktrees, dispatched fixes and re-merged. True only under --approve and only when UAT failed with attempt budget remaining.

advanced.description: True when this attempt's UAT passed for every target, so no further fix was needed.
